message:
fix(time): readiness-waker pattern prevents spurious re-polls and timer-fired uses max(now, deadline)

Sleep gains a ReadyWaker wrapper that sets an atomic ready flag before
delegating to the inner waker. poll_with_time checks this flag first
(via swap to prevent double-fire), so a Sleep that was already woken
by a timer driver completes immediately without re-checking the clock.
This prevents a race where the timer fires between the now() read and
the deadline comparison, causing the Sleep to return Pending despite
being ready.

The ready flag is reset on reset() and reset_to() to ensure a reset
Sleep re-enters the waiting state cleanly.

Timer-fired trace events now use max(now, deadline) as the timestamp
instead of now, ensuring the trace shows the timer firing at or after
its deadline even if the runtime processes the event slightly before
the deadline due to clock granularity.

TimerDriver::update now explicitly cancels the active handle before
registering the new one, with a fail-closed path if the supplied
handle is stale/inactive.

VirtualTimerWheel: minor timer wheel adjustment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
